summary: Iteration 15 REFACTOR verification GREEN across the board. server.py dropped 4703 -> 1960 lines with routes split into /app/backend/routers/{auth,ops,telehealth,forms_protocols,compliance}.py. All /api/* paths remain identical. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_iter15_refactor.py (23 tests covering all 5 extracted routers) — 23/23 PASS. Re-ran /app/backend/tests/test_phase15_hipaa.py — 18/18 PASS. Total backend: 41/41 GREEN. Frontend smoke: (1) All 4 role logins land on correct portals (admin -> /portal/admin, practitioner -> /portal/provider, staff -> /portal/staff, AUDITOR -> /portal/admin/audit with the Audit Log UI fully rendering — the iteration_14 infinite redirect loop is FIXED thanks to Protected.jsx auditor break-glass passthrough). (2) Zero 'Maximum update depth exceeded' console errors for auditor login. (3) MyAccount password label reads '(12+ chars, cannot contain your name/email, no common passwords)' — copy fix verified. (4) MyDataExportCard uses ES import for useToast; both my-data-download-btn (POST /patient/data-export -> 200 + JSON downloaded) and my-disclosures-btn (GET /clients/{me}/disclosures -> 200) work end-to-end with zero console errors. (5) models.py Field(min_length=1) confirmed on both UserCreate.password (line 47) and PasswordChange.new_password (line 616) — NIST validator now emits clean 400 'Password must be at least 12 characters.' on the change-password endpoint (verified via test_change_password_rejects_short).
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is clean — single shared api = APIRouter(prefix='/api') in deps.py, each router imports it and registers handlers via decorators. server.py's `from routers import auth as _auth_routes` side-effect pattern works, but a follow-up cleanup could switch to explicit `api.include_router(sub_router)` per module for clearer dependency graphs and easier per-domain testing.

server.py is still 1960 lines and holds Clients/Intake/Notes/Files/Appointments/Availability/Memberships/Invoices/Plans/Reminders/Symptoms/Labs/Messages/Public/Dashboard/Admin — those are 14+ resource domains that could be split further (target <500 lines per file). The main win is done though; incremental extraction is fine.

deps.get_current_user + require_roles + _resolve_self_client + _strip_id are now the sole shared surface — good. If a new router forgets to depend on get_current_user, all routes silently become public. Consider a smoke test that iterates api.routes and asserts every non-/public/* route has a Depends(get_current_user) or Depends(require_roles(...)) in its signature.

PasswordChange model with Field(min_length=1) is intentional (validator is single source of truth). Add a code comment on models.py:46 and :616 explaining why min_length=1 and NOT the intuitive 12 — otherwise a future dev will 'helpfully' bump it back to 12 and re-trigger the 422-vs-400 discrepancy.

Protected.jsx auditor passthrough (roles.includes('client') gate) is a nice targeted fix; consider adding a runtime warning in dev if any route in App.js has roles={['client', 'admin']} together — that combination would open the auditor to the patient portal too, which is not desired.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor + iteration 14 carry-over fixes are all verified GREEN. New regression file /app/backend/tests/test_iter15_refactor.py covers the extracted routers — re-run it after any future router.py edits. Phase 15 HIPAA suite unchanged (18/18). Frontend smoke: to reproduce auditor flow, login auditor@natmedsol.local / Auditor!2345 -> lands at /portal/admin/audit with Audit Log UI rendering, no console spam. Patient MyAccount data-export card works end-to-end (POST /patient/data-export -> 200 JSON, GET /clients/{me}/disclosures -> 200 JSON, both triggered by data-testid=my-data-download-btn and my-disclosures-btn).
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration verifies fixes to the iteration_14 issues (auditor loop, min_length=8 vs 12 mismatch, MyAccount label, require() vs ES import) AND validates the 4703 -> 1960 line server.py refactor. All previously-flagged bugs are GREEN.
